hero.badge: <svg viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><path d="M12 22s8-4 8-10V5l-8-3-8 3v7c0 6 8 10 8 10z"/></svg> Free app — Windows, macOS, Linux
hero.title: Your eyes, spine and mind will thank you
hero.subtitle: Smart breaks, hydration reminders, eye exercises and activity tracking — all in one lightweight app that runs in the background.
feat.title: What does HealthDesk do?
feat.subtitle: Key features + more — everything you need for healthy computer work.
feat.breaks.d: 4 work methods to choose from: Pomodoro, 20-20-20, 52-17 and 90-minute. Smart break protection — reminders never overlap.
feat.water.d: Goal: 8 glasses a day. Gentle reminders every 30 minutes. Track progress and build a habit.
feat.eyes.d: Guided sessions with a timer. Eye movements, blinking, focus changes — reduce eye strain.
feat.track.d: Automatic app categorization into work, entertainment, communication and learning.
feat.audio.d: Brown noise, rain, forest, ocean — generated in real time.
feat.radio.d: 6 YT stations + 6 FM stations (RMF, Antyradio, ZET, Eska). Pause stops music together with timers.
feat.breath.d: Box breathing 4-4-4-4 with an animated circle. Reduce stress in 60 seconds.
feat.stretch.d: Guided exercises for neck, shoulders and back — step by step with a timer.
feat.idle.d: Detects inactivity and Focus Assist. Timers freeze when you step away. Zero popups when Windows DND is active.
preview.title: See how it looks
preview.subtitle: Dark, elegant interface that doesn't distract — delivers information at a glance.
how.title: How does it work?
how.subtitle: Three simple steps to healthier computer work.
how.s1.d: Download the installer, click next and you're done. No account, no sign-up.
how.s2.d: HealthDesk runs in the background. You won't notice it until it's time for a break.
how.s3.d: Respond to reminders. Your eyes and spine will thank you.
why.title: Why is it worth it?
why.subtitle: Habits backed by scientific research.
why.s1.d: Short breaks every 25–52 minutes increase productivity by 13% and reduce mental fatigue.
why.s2.d: Regular hydration improves focus and reduces headaches.
why.s3.d: Short movement breaks prevent back pain and improve circulation.
why.s3.src: Source: WHO Guidelines on Physical Activity (2020)
cmp.title: How does HealthDesk compare?
cmp.subtitle: Honest comparison with popular alternatives.
cmp.footer: Data based on versions available in February 2026. Size and RAM may vary by platform.
cta.title: Ready for healthier work?
cta.subtitle: Install for free and start taking care of yourself — from the very first break.
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.a1: Yes, completely free. No hidden fees, subscriptions or premium. All features available right away.
faq.q2: Does the app collect my data?
faq.a2: Statistics, breaks and hydration are stored locally on your computer. The app sends anonymous telemetry (installation UUID, usage events, system version) to improve the product. You can disable this in Settings > System > Anonymous telemetry. We do not collect any personal data.
faq.a3: Yes — breaks, exercises, water, activity and sounds work offline. Only YouTube Radio requires internet.
faq.a4: Yes. Choose a preset work method (Pomodoro, 20-20-20, 52-17, 90-minute) or set custom intervals. Break frequency, water reminders, work hours — all configurable in settings.
faq.q5: How do I uninstall the app?
faq.q7: Windows shows a blue screen during installation?
modal.title: Before installing
modal.subtitle: The app is safe and open source. Your system may require an extra step.
mock.break_desc:
Look away from the screen and focus
on an object about 6 meters away.
faq.a5:
Windows: Settings > Apps > HealthDesk > Uninstall.
macOS: Move HealthDesk from Applications to Trash. Data: ~/Library/Application Support/HealthDesk/.
Linux: sudo apt remove healthdesk (for .deb) or delete the .AppImage file. Data: ~/.config/HealthDesk/.

User data (settings, statistics) remains after uninstalling — you can delete it manually.
faq.a6: The Windows installer is only ~4 MB, and the app uses ~16 MB RAM after installation. HealthDesk 2.0 was rewritten using Tauri (Rust + React), ensuring native performance and minimal size.
faq.a7:
That's Windows SmartScreen — it appears for any new app without a commercial code signing certificate. HealthDesk is 100% safe and open source.

How to install:
1. Click "More info"
2. Click "Run anyway"
3. Done — installation will proceed normally

The message will disappear automatically once the app gains enough downloads and reputation in Microsoft's system.
modal.win.note: SmartScreen appears for any new app without a commercial certificate. This is normal.
modal.mac.s1: Download the .dmg file and open it
modal.mac.s4: Confirm by clicking "Open" in the dialog
modal.mac.note: macOS Gatekeeper blocks apps from outside the App Store. Right-click > Open bypasses this.
modal.linux.note: Linux has no SmartScreen equivalent — installation requires no extra steps.
feat.audioradio.d: 6 native sounds (brown noise, rain, forest) + YouTube Radio + 6 FM stations. Pause stops music with timers.
feat.exercises.d: Breathing, eyes, stretching — animated sessions with timer. Box breathing 4-4-4-4, neck, shoulder and back exercises.
feat.calendar.d: Meeting sync — block breaks during meetings, pre-meeting reminder, automatic break after long meetings.
feat.schedule.d: Separate break profile for each day — e.g. shorter intervals on Monday, longer on Friday. Day timeline on dashboard.
faq.a8: HealthDesk runs in the background and doesn't interfere until it's time for a break. Gentle mode shows a small non-blocking window — you can ignore it. With Google Calendar integration, breaks automatically skip meetings.
faq.q9: I already have a phone timer — why do I need HealthDesk?
faq.a9: A phone timer doesn't track activity, guide eye exercises, know about your calendar meetings, pause when you step away, or play ambient sounds. HealthDesk is a complete wellness system, not just a timer.
faq.q10: Does it slow down my computer?
faq.a10: HealthDesk uses ~30 MB RAM — less than a single browser tab. The installer is only ~4 MB. The app is built with Rust (backend) + React (UI), ensuring native performance.
cta.demo: Or try the demo in your browser
demo.meta_desc: Try HealthDesk directly in your browser. Interactive demo with desktop simulation, break timers, breathing exercises and sound generator.
demo.banner: This is an interactive HealthDesk demo. Some features are simulated.
demo.tooltip_activity: In the full version, HealthDesk automatically categorizes apps into work, entertainment, communication and learning
demo.tooltip_radio: In the full version, listen to real radio stations via HTTP stream
demo.tooltip_yt: In the full version, search any music on YouTube — playback via yt-dlp
demo.tooltip_yt_custom: In the full version, paste any YouTube link and listen to music
demo.work_method_desc: Choose a preset method or customize
demo.daily_goal_desc: How many glasses of water per day
demo.tooltip_gcal: In the full version, connect to Google Calendar — breaks automatically skip meetings, get pre-meeting reminders
demo.sys_tray_desc: HealthDesk runs in the background from the system tray icon. Quick access to pause, water and music without opening the main window.
demo.tooltip_tray: HealthDesk runs in the background from the system tray icon — quick access to pause, water and music
demo.telemetry_desc: Anonymous usage data helps improve the app
demo.faq1_q: How do breaks work in HealthDesk?
demo.faq1_a: HealthDesk monitors work time and automatically reminds you about eye breaks (every 20 min) and movement breaks (every 60 min). Intervals depend on the chosen work method — 20-20-20, Pomodoro, 52-17 or custom.
demo.faq2_a: Yes! In Settings > Breaks you can choose a preset method or set custom intervals. You can also adjust break mode — from gentle notification to full-screen enforcement.
demo.faq3_a: Box breathing is a relaxation technique: 4s inhale, 4s hold, 4s exhale, 4s hold. HealthDesk guides you through 5 cycles with an animated indicator.
demo.faq4_q: How does Google Calendar integration work?
demo.faq4_a: After connecting your account, HealthDesk automatically detects meetings and freezes timers during them. It also sends pre-meeting reminders and accelerates breaks after long meetings.
demo.faq5_a: HealthDesk only collects anonymous usage statistics (with your consent). Window activity data stays exclusively on your computer in a local SQLite database.
demo.faq6_q: What systems does HealthDesk run on?
demo.faq6_a: HealthDesk works on Windows 10/11, macOS (Apple Silicon + Intel) and Linux (Ubuntu, Fedora, Arch). The app uses ~30 MB RAM and is available for free.
